figures.caption: FIG. 2. Magnon-driven anomalous Hall effect in an altermagnetic minimal model. (a) The lattice structure. (b) Band structure of the minimal model in the absence of spin-orbit coupling. The k-path are −M ′ = (−0.5, 0, 0.25), Γ′ = (0, 0, 0.25), and M ′ = (0.5, 0, 0.25). (c), (d) Spin-up and spin-down Fermi surfaces in kx − ky plane at kz = π/2 (without spin-orbit coupling), together with the Berry curvature Ωz(k) in (c) and the magnon-driven anomalous Hall response coefficient χ̃zz(k) in (d) (arbitrary units). (e) Magnon-driven anomalous Hall coefficient χ̃zz as a function of Fermi energy with the Néel vector along the z direction. (f) Angular dependence of χ̃sep xz and χ̃sep zz as the Néel vector rotates from the z to x axis, parameterized by the angle θ. The response coefficient is measured in units of c0S/cm, with c0 = τ0/~. The inset shows the Néel-vector direction. Model parameters are t2/t1 = 1.0, t3/t1 = 0.5, µ/t1 = 0.5, λ/t1 = 0.004, and J/t1 = 0.45. The magnon frequency is ω/t1 = 0.1. The Fermi energy is fixed at EF /t1 = 0 in (f).
